description: The writer addressing his first cousin, a woman, who probably was his in-law, expresses the wish that the extended family may be reunited in one place. He assures the mother of his wife that her daughter lives in comfort and asks her to send her daughter some of the copper vessels and clothing, parts of the dowry, which had not been delivered yet. (Information from Mediterranean Society, IV, p. 297; V, pp. 47, 525)
description_he: הכותב פונה לבת דודתו, ייתכן שהיא גם חמותו, ומביע תקווה שהמשפחה המורחבת תתאחד במקום אחד. הוא מבטיח לאם אשתו שבתה חיה ברווחה, ומבקש לשלוח לבתה כלים מנחושת ובגדים שטרם נמסרו מהנדוניה.
